content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "The-Lorax". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/gym/102694/problem/F
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of trees. The second line contains n integers, representing the heights of the trees.
content.outputFormat: The output should be the maximum number of trees that can be cut down.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the heights of the trees and then cut down the trees with the smallest heights first.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
mid = n // 2
count = 0
for i in range(mid):
    if arr[i] < arr[mid]:
        count += 1
print(count)
